name: Nifty 50
summary: Day 75 of Hormuz blockade. **DAY 2 CASCADE EXTENSION — Sensex CLOSED MAY 12 AT 74,559.24 (-1,456 PTS, -1.92%) = LARGEST 2-SESSION DRAWDOWN OF CYCLE; cumulative ~16 LAKH CRORE m-cap erosion across 4 sessions; Nifty 50 CLOSED 23,379.55 (-436.3 pts, -1.83%), BROKE BELOW 23,500 SUPPORT that had held since May 5-6 stabilisation. Decline broad — IT, realty, consumer durables led (IT sold on AI-pricing-pressure overhang in addition to crisis flow).** **MOODY'S CUT INDIA FY26 GROWTH 0.8PP TO 6.0% (from 6.8%)** = SOVEREIGN-RATING actor (materially harder than yesterday's brokerage 6.7%→6.2% flag). USD/INR ₹95.63 fresh CLOSING record. May 13 intraday OPENED with relief bid: Sensex +700 from low, Nifty above 23,450 per BS LIVE morning (close prints in markets dossier: Nifty 23,496.80 +0.5%, Sensex 74,813.40 +0.3%, Bank Nifty 53,974.00 +0.8%) — but intraday recovery from May 12 collapse, NOT a regime reversal; cumulative drawdown still -1.83% from May 11 peak. **Hardeep Puri quantified OMC pain: PSU OMCs LOSING ₹1,000 CRORE/DAY, total under-recovery on track ₹1.98 lakh crore.** India reintroduced WFH rules as fuel-conservation measure (Fortune). **Iran FM Araghchi arrives New Delhi May 13 for BRICS FM meet May 14-15; meets Modi May 14 same day Trump in Beijing; Modi 5-nation tour May 15-20 (UAE first) — most overt Indian strategic-autonomy choreography of cycle.** India 10Y G-Sec 6.96-7.00% est. flat (Moody's cut absorbed). Sectoral: PSU OMC continuing under-recovery pressure; Reliance refining margin tailwind; Nifty IT -3% May 12 intraday; Nifty Pharma -3% YTD vs Nifty -12% YTD. **STRUCTURAL STORY: US (S&P 500 -0.29% off record on CPI absorption) + Korea (KOSPI +2.8% bounce after May 12 -1.7%) running their own cycles while India takes whole brunt of energy-cascade — worst import-FII-currency triple-vulnerability of major economies. Moody's cut adds sovereign-rating channel.** Reversibility ASYMMETRIC: requires (a) Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions AND (b) FII positive AND (c) INR ₹93 — none imminent. ORIGINAL CONTEXT (May 12): **MAJOR MARKET REPRICING — Sensex MAY 11 CLOSED 76,015.28 (-1,312.91 pts, -1.70%) = LARGEST SINGLE-DAY POINT DROP OF CYCLE; YTD drop crossed 10,000 points; Nifty 23,815.85 (-360.30 / -1.49% May 11) → 23,578.00 May 12 (-1.0% 1D); Bank Nifty 54,439.90 May 11 (-870.65) → V-shape May 12 recovery 56,565.70 intraday (+0.85%) → 53,914.00 close (-1.0% 1D, -1.7% 1M, -11.2% 3M).** **FII SINGLE-DAY NET SELL ₹8,437.56 cr MAY 11 (highest since April 24); DII +₹5,939.65 cr absorbed ~70%; YTD CY26 FII outflow ₹2.06 LAKH CR (exceeds full-year 2025 ₹1.66 lakh cr by ₹40k cr in 2 sessions of +₹14k).** Drivers: Trump publicly rejected Iran counter-proposal ('TOTALLY UNACCEPTABLE'/'garbage') + Brent broke $105 + USD/INR ₹95.54 fresh record breaking RBI ₹95.50 threshold + WH Sit-Room weighing renewed strikes. **Sectoral repricing:** PSU OMCs (BPCL -2.34%, HPCL -2.65%, IOC -2.45% May 11) face Q1 FY27 losses large enough to wipe full-year FY26 profits per brokerage flags; Reliance May 12 intraday +1.99% (refining margin tailwind diverges from OMC marketing-margin compression); Nifty IT -3% May 12 intraday (US-growth fears overwhelm rupee tailwind); Nifty Pharma -3% YTD vs Nifty -12% YTD (defensive bid intact). India 10Y G-Sec 6.96-6.99%; new 10Y auction cut-off near 6.95% (auction-anchored despite crude bid). **DECOUPLING: US S&P 500 fresh record close +0.2% / NASDAQ +0.1% / Dow +0.2% — US essentially decouples from war while India takes whole brunt.** PM Modi May 10-11 Hyderabad seven-appeal address (defer gold + foreign travel + cut fertilizer 50% + edible oil + fuel + swadeshi) targets FX pressure. Modi 5-nation tour May 15-20 (UAE-Netherlands-Sweden-Norway-Italy) = diversification play. FY27 GDP cut 6.7%→6.2% (global brokerage; 'historically large energy shock'). Bearish scenario: ₹96-97 INR + Nifty toward 22,000 if no deal + kinetic escalation. Reversibility ASYMMETRIC: requires Brent <$95 sustained 5+ sessions + FII positive + ₹93 INR — none imminent.

current.deployment_zone: India taking WHOLE BRUNT of energy-cascade pressure. FII -₹8,437 cr May 11 (highest since April 24); ₹2.06 lakh cr YTD exceeds full-year 2025 by ₹40k cr. PSU OMC Q1 FY27 losses could wipe full-year FY26 profits per brokerage flags. US decoupling structural. Bearish ₹96-97 INR + Nifty 22,000 if no deal. Bullish requires Brent <$95 sustained + FII positive + ₹93 INR (Goldman 2026 average $85 implicitly requires Q4 $71 = 'back half of year' setup).

recent_signals.headline: MAJOR REPRICING: Sensex May 11 closed 76,015.28 (-1,312.91 pts, -1.70%) = LARGEST SINGLE-DAY POINT DROP OF CYCLE, YTD drop crossed 10,000 pts; Nifty 23,815.85 (-360.30, -1.49%) → 23,578.00 May 12 close (-1.0%); Bank Nifty 54,439.90 May 11 (-870.65) → V-shape May 12 recovery to 56,565.70 intraday (+0.85%) → 53,914.00 close. FII single-day net sell ₹8,437.56 cr May 11 (highest since April 24); DII +₹5,939.65 cr absorbed ~70%; YTD CY26 FII outflow ₹2.06 LAKH CR (exceeds full-year 2025 ₹1.66 lakh cr by ₹40k cr). USD/INR ₹95.54 fresh record. Sectoral: PSU OMCs hit hard (BPCL -2.34%, HPCL -2.65%, IOC -2.45%); Reliance May 12 intraday +1.99% (refining margin tailwind); Nifty IT -3% May 12 intraday (US-growth fears); Nifty Pharma -3% YTD vs Nifty -12% YTD (defensive intact). India 10Y G-Sec 6.96-6.99%; new 10Y auction cut-off 6.95%. **DECOUPLING: US S&P fresh record (+0.2%, 7,412.84) / NASDAQ +0.1% (26,274.13) / Dow +0.2% / VIX +6.9% to 18.38 — India takes whole brunt.** Modi May 10-11 seven-appeal address + Modi 5-nation tour May 15-20. FY27 GDP cut 6.7%→6.2%.
sources: Business Standard May 11 + May 12 live blogs (primary)

recent_signals.headline: SUNDAY HOLIDAY — NIFTY 24,176.15 / SENSEX 77,328.19 / BANK NIFTY 55,310.55 (Friday May 8 close still operative). India taking it while global equities make records: S&P record +0.84%, NASDAQ +1.7%, KOSPI +0.1% at +36.5% MoM, MSCI EM +2.0%. FII -₹340.89 cr May 8 vs DII +₹441 cr; ₹1.92 LAKH CR YTD CY26 already exceeds full-year 2025 (₹1.66 lakh cr). FPI ownership ~16%, lowest in 2 decades. Bank Nifty -1.3% leading = financials pricing NIM compression + FII channel withdrawal. **Monday May 11 open will be first re-pricing event for accumulated unpriced news (Sunday Doha strike, CENTCOM 58/4, Qatar PM Miami, Bahrain detentions, Kharg slick, UAE engagement scale).** If Brent gaps up $2-4 Monday, Nifty likely opens softer; if MOU signature signal emerges Sunday, gap-up possible. 200-DMA ~24,500 still capping; structural breakout requires (a) MOU signature within Trump 1-week window (closes May 14) or May 21 Sadjadpour test, (b) FII rotation reversal, (c) ₹93-handle USD/INR.
